data.narrative:
Firehawk Aerospace is a Series A-stage defense and security company that has raised $25M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 10 comparable companies across the sector—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms—including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries, and NODA AI at the same stage.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $25M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $50M. Peer-median analysis references four same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $225M, though the peer range spans $125M to $500M, reflecting meaningful dispersion in this cohort. The peer-median approach is weighted given the direct comparability of the sample.

The triangulated valuation range is $42M (low) to $259M (high), with a median of $138M. The wide spread reflects the inherent uncertainty in early-stage defense-tech valuations, where peer company trajectories, customer concentration, and regulatory pathways vary substantially. Key unknowns include Firehawk's revenue traction, customer pipeline quality, and technical differentiation relative to the peer set. The median of $138M represents a reasonable central estimate pending deeper diligence on these operational drivers.
